title: The *R for Mass Spectrometry* project and meta-package
text: RforMassSpectrometry meta-package, manage document R Mass Spectrometry (https://www.rformassspectrometry.org/) project. goal project provide efficient, thoroughly documented, tested flexible R software analysis interpretation high throughput mass spectrometry assays, including proteomics metabolomics experiments. project formalises longtime collaborative development efforts members R Mass Spectrometry organisation facilitate dissemination accessibility work.

title: The *R for Mass Spectrometry* project and meta-package
text: install core R Mass Spectrometry packages, need BiocManager package, available CRAN. already available, install proceed installation, run Note recent Bioconductor needed install run suite R Mass Spectrometry packages, require recent version R possibly even installing Bioconductor development version. Loading RforMassSpectrometry package load required core packages.
code: if (!require("BiocManager"))     install.package("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("RforMassSpectrometry/RforMassSpectrometry") library("RforMassSpectrometry") ## Warning: replacing previous import 'MsCoreUtils::entropy' by 'Spectra::entropy' ## when loading 'RforMassSpectrometry' ## Warning: replacing previous import 'MsCoreUtils::bin' by 'Spectra::bin' when ## loading 'RforMassSpectrometry' ## Warning: replacing previous import 'MsCoreUtils::smooth' by 'Spectra::smooth' ## when loading 'RforMassSpectrometry' ##  ## Visit https://RforMassSpectrometry.org for detail about the project.

title: The *R for Mass Spectrometry* project and meta-package
text: R Mass Spectrometry project welcomes contributions form ideas, documentation, code, packages, fixes (bugs typos), … pull requests (PR). invite discuss opening GitHub issue relevant package github repository. direct contributions existing package, suggest first discuss dedicted issue implies substantial changes (need issue simple typo, really appreciate ). non-trivial portion contribution copied somewhere else (AI-generated copied another software project) required discuss issue disclose PR description provide details provenance (example, ‘Assisted-: Name AI’ ‘Code copied : source’). Make sure cite provenance new code newly contributed code can redistributed software/packages licensing terms. Contributions, even core members, always expected come form PR undergoes formal review. , start forking package want contribute. also advised work , make PR, non-master branch. Good branch names include issueX relates specific issue, feat-X, bug-Y, docs-Z new features, bug fixes documentation updates respectively. reviewers might ask additional clarifications /changes prior merging PR. contemplating contributing code, please follow style guide . particular, several developers collaborate feature, admin create dedicated branch RforMassSpectrometry/Package:branch. Developers fetch branch local copy Package repository send PRs branch , reviewed, eventually merged RforMassSpectrometry/Package:master. also anticipate open specific requests contributions invite specific members community, identify specific need /specific developer address need. R Mass Spectrometry packages released open licences. contributions adhere . guarantee provenance legality contribution, please submit won’t able accept . Reviewers best help get PR merged1. See also tidyverse contribution guide additional resources instructions.

title: The *R for Mass Spectrometry* project and meta-package
text: Commit messages follow Conventional Commits guidelines. particular, following types recommended (based angulars types): build: Changes affect build system external dependencies (example scopes: depends, suggests, imports, …). ci: Changes continous integration (CI) configuration files scripts (example: Travis configuration file). docs: Documentation changes, including manual pages vignettes. feat: new feature. fix: bug fix. perf: code change improves performance. refactor: code change neither fixes bug adds feature. style: Changes affect meaning code (white-space, formatting, …). test: Adding missing tests correcting existing tests. : Anything doesn’t fit . Consider opening issue suggestion new type.

title: The *R for Mass Spectrometry* project and meta-package
text: also want offer proper credit provide substantial contributions. Substantial contributions include, example new functionality goes beyond e.g. simple plot function bug fixes combined related unit tests extended documentation, examples vignettes describing complete (potentially alternative) analysis workflow use-case Fixing typo(s) isn’t considered substantial contribution per-se, even though highly appreciated. substantial contribution passes review merged, add contributor package’s author list official contributor (ctb role) even author (aut role) substantial contributions. contributors name also added R Mass Spectrometry web page relevant publications directly stem work.

title: The *R for Mass Spectrometry* project and meta-package
text: R Mass Spectrometry packages follows Bioconductor style guide. particular use . naming symbols. leading . can used hidden/local functions variables. Snake case restricted internal functions. consistency, favour camel case public functions. Class names start capital class posses constructor identical name. Running constructor without input produce valid empty object. Use ## start full-line comments. roxygen headers #' preferred, although ##' tolerated. Use spaces = function arguments class definition: f(= 1, b = 2). Always use space comma: , b, c. Always use spaces around binary operators: + b. Lines kept shorter 80 characters. example following code isn’t accepted wrapped shown :
code: # no wrap at 80 someVeryLongVariableName <- someVeryLongFunctionName(withSomeEvenLongerFunctionArgumentA = 1, withSomeEvenLongerFunctionArgumentB = 2) # alternative 1 someVeryLongVariableName <-     someVeryLongFunctionName(withSomeEvenLongerFunctionArgumentA = 1,                              withSomeEvenLongerFunctionArgumentB = 2) # alternative 2 someVeryLongVariableName <- someVeryLongFunctionName(     withSomeEvenLongerFunctionArgumentA = 1,     withSomeEvenLongerFunctionArgumentB = 2)
